focus: Concrete literal command bodies and content ownership matrix
noveltyJustification: 3 of 5 findings were fully new and 2 partially new (0.80), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for resolving two open questions with one ownership model.
keyQuestions: What complete literal body should each of the five commands contain?
Which content stays literal, shared, or mode-owned?
answeredQuestions: What complete literal body should each of the five commands contain?
Which content stays literal, shared, or mode-owned?
ruledOut: Copying mode-owned taste, reference inventories, or transport mechanics into commands
Duplicating the complete shared lifecycle in five bodies
Applying anti-generic creation rhetoric to source-faithful extraction
Replacing useful route, suffix, sibling, and typed-status architecture

summary: Literal command bodies should own the mode-specific mission, progressive intake, ordered work, critique rule, evidence ceiling, and typed product while delegating taste.
One canonical @ expansion carries shared lifecycle and schemas without five-copy drift.
A three-way placement rule separates literal public orchestration, shared normative schemas, and mode-owned judgment.
All five bodies can share one structural grammar while retaining distinct decisive quality rules.
Design-reference substitutes source fidelity and provenance for anti-generic creative originality.
